calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-22-2026 Continued To Jun-30-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND Amended COMPLAINT.

Appearances (in person):
Henry A. Wirta, Jr., Esq., of Harrington, Foxx, Dubrow & Canter, LLP, (415) 565-9600, for Plaintiff.

Nathaniel S.G. Braun, Esq., of Sinclair Braun Kargher LLP, (213) 429-6100, for Defendant.

Not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Off calendar in light of the ruling on Progressive's demurrer.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-17-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT.

Appearances (in person):
Henry A. Wirta, Jr., Esq., of Harrington, Foxx, Dubrow & Canter, LLP, (415) 565-9600, for Plaintiff.

Nathaniel S.G. Braun, Esq., of Sinclair Braun Kargher LLP, (213) 429-6100, for Defendant.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Progressive Casualty Insurance Company's demurrer to the sole cause of action for bad faith insurance practice in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff D&M Carriers LLC is sustained without leave to amend. Insurance Code 790.03, the sole legal basis for D&M's sole claim, does not create a private right of action. Because D&M has not shown that there is a reasonable possibility that it can allege a viable claim against Progressive, D&M's request for leave to amend is denied. D&M's apparent acknowledgment that Progressive did not issue a policy covering the November 30, 2023 Oklahoma City incident shows that D&M will not be able to allege a viable claim against Progressive.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept.301 a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/HEK)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-15-2026 Continued To Apr-28-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2026 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant Progressive Casualty Insurance Company's motion to strike portions of the first amended complaint filed by D&M Carriers, LLC is off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on Progressive Casualty Insurance Company's demurrer to the first amended complaint.

Visting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-12-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendant Progressive Casualty Insurance Company's demurrer to the sole cause of action in the first amended complaint filed by D&M Carriers, LLC is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. If it can do so in good faith and in accordance with applicable law and the facts as it understands them, D&M has leave to file a second amended complaint setting forth one or more viable claims against Progressive Casualty Insurance Company. The sole cause of action alleged in the first amended complaint assumes that an insurance policy issued by Progressive Casualty Insurance Company provided defense and indemnity coverage for the November 30, 2023 accident yet D&M's opposition to this demurrer argues that whether Progressive Casualty Insurance Company issued such a policy "misses the point." Under these unusual circumstances, the sole cause of action in the first amended complaint is contradicted by the D'&M's own filing and fails to state a claim.

Proposed order submitted.

Visting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
